data.content:
## Build Failure Diagnosis

**Solution:** `BuildDiag.sln` — 3 projects: `Core` → `Services` → `Api`

### Root Cause (1 of 1)

**Missing NuGet package in `Services/Services.csproj`**

`Services/UserService.cs` uses `Newtonsoft.Json` (`JObject`, etc.) but `Services.csproj` has **no `<PackageReference>` for `Newtonsoft.Json`** — only a `<ProjectReference>` to `Core`. The `project.assets.json` confirms Newtonsoft.Json is not resolved.

All 3 errors stem from this single root cause:

| Error | File | Line | Description |
|-------|------|------|-------------|
| CS0246 | `UserService.cs` | 2:7 | `using Newtonsoft.Json` — namespace not found |
| CS0246 | `UserService.cs` | 3:7 | `using Newtonsoft.Json.Linq` — namespace not found |
| CS0246 | `UserService.cs` | 22:19 | `JObject` type not found (consequence of above) |

### Cascading Failure

`Api.csproj` depends on `Services.csproj` via `<ProjectReference>`. Because `Services` failed to compile, **`Api` never reached its own Csc compilation** — its build was skipped entirely. The `Api` project itself has no errors of its own.

### Fix

Add the missing NuGet package reference to `Services/Services.csproj`:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This single change will resolve all 3 errors and unblock the `Api` project build.
